A.label: Direct match
A.description: Perioperative cN1 M0 MIBC, cystectomy planned

B.label: Partial perioperative match
B.description: Perioperative MIBC with some cN1 or strong nodal subgroup

B-.label: Nodal but wrong setting
B-.description: LN-only metastatic or perioperative wrong population

trials.setting: Neoadjuvant EV+pembro → radical cystectomy → adjuvant pembro
trials.population: Locally advanced and/or node-positive MIBC (cT2-T4 N1-N3 M0)
eligibility_highlights: cT2-T4 N1-N3 M0 or cT1 N2-N3 M0
Node positivity: biopsy preferred; imaging ≥1.0 cm if no biopsy
trials.limitation_for_patient: Phase 2 only; no published efficacy; reimbursement uncertain
trials.interpretation: Only prospective trial designed specifically for node-positive perioperative EV+pembro. Best conceptual match but immature evidence.

what_we_know: Only prospective trial built for node-positive MIBC with EV+pembro before cystectomy
Allows cT2–T4 N1–N3 M0; cisplatin-eligible and -ineligible
Primary endpoint pCR; recruiting, no published efficacy yet

subgroup_outcomes.headline: cN1–N3 cM0, perioperative before RC
what_we_know: Only prospective trial built for node-positive MIBC with EV+pembro before cystectomy
Allows cT2–T4 N1–N3 M0; cisplatin-eligible and -ineligible
Primary endpoint pCR; recruiting, no published efficacy yet

trials.setting: First-line locally advanced or metastatic urothelial carcinoma
trials.limitation_for_patient: Metastatic/unresectable setting — not perioperative curative pathway before cystectomy
trials.interpretation: Strongest proof EV+pembro beats chemotherapy in nodal urothelial disease (n=207), but wrong clinical setting for curative cN1 M0 pre-cystectomy.

what_we_know: 207 patients with lymph-node-only advanced disease
ORR 77.5% vs 53.4%; PFS HR 0.47; OS HR 0.51
Strongest EV+pembro vs chemo data in nodal urothelial cancer
what_we_dont_know: Applicability to curative perioperative cN1 M0 pathway
pN0 or pCR after cystectomy — wrong treatment paradigm

subgroup_outcomes.headline: LN-only metastatic/unresectable (not perioperative cN1 M0)
what_we_know: 207 patients with lymph-node-only advanced disease
ORR 77.5% vs 53.4%; PFS HR 0.47; OS HR 0.51
Strongest EV+pembro vs chemo data in nodal urothelial cancer
what_we_dont_know: Applicability to curative perioperative cN1 M0 pathway
pN0 or pCR after cystectomy — wrong treatment paradigm

trials.limitation_for_patient: Durvalumab+chemo not EV+pembro; cN1 subgroup benefit not proven
trials.interpretation: Strongest approved perioperative alternative if cisplatin-fit. EAU Category 1. cN1 n=58 with NS subgroup result.

what_we_know: Approved perioperative durvalumab + GC; n=1063 total
cN1 subgroup n=58: EFS HR 0.75 (95% CI 0.22–1.64) — not significant
Overall EFS HR 0.68, pCR 37.3% vs 27.5%
what_we_dont_know: pN0 rate specifically in cN1 patients

subgroup_outcomes.headline: cN1 at baseline (cisplatin-eligible perioperative)
what_we_know: Approved perioperative durvalumab + GC; n=1063 total
cN1 subgroup n=58: EFS HR 0.75 (95% CI 0.22–1.64) — not significant
Overall EFS HR 0.68, pCR 37.3% vs 27.5%
what_we_dont_know: pN0 rate specifically in cN1 patients

trials.population: T2-T4aN0M0 or T1-T4aN1M0; cisplatin-ineligible or declined

efficacy.population_note: >80% cisplatin-ineligible per Galsky criteria; median age ~74

trials.limitation_for_patient: Cisplatin-ineligible population; cN1 only ~17 patients; not applicable if cisplatin-fit
trials.interpretation: Definitive perioperative EV+pembro phase 3, but wrong population if she is cisplatin-eligible. Tiny cN1 subset.

patient_match.certainty_label: Tiny cN1 subset; wrong population if cis-fit
what_we_know: Phase 3 perioperative EV+pembro vs RC alone in cis-ineligible MIBC
Overall EFS HR 0.40, pCR 57.1%
~17 cN1 patients — insufficient for subgroup conclusions

subgroup_outcomes.headline: T1–T4aN1 (4.9%); cisplatin-INELIGIBLE population
subgroup_outcomes.certainty_label: Tiny cN1 subset; wrong population if cis-fit
what_we_know: Phase 3 perioperative EV+pembro vs RC alone in cis-ineligible MIBC
Overall EFS HR 0.40, pCR 57.1%
~17 cN1 patients — insufficient for subgroup conclusions

trials.setting: Neoadjuvant MVAC → cystectomy vs cystectomy alone

efficacy.note: 5-year OS benefit with NAC in N0 MIBC — foundational trial

trials.limitation_for_patient: Enrolled cN0 only — no direct cN1 evidence
trials.interpretation: Czech doctors' historical standard. Cannot directly support or refute cN1 NAC from this RCT.

patient_match.n_label: cN0 only — no node-positive patients enrolled
what_we_know: Foundational NAC RCT in MIBC (MVAC → RC)
Established OS benefit for NAC in N0 MIBC
what_we_dont_know: Any direct evidence for cN1 M0 — not enrolled

subgroup_outcomes.headline: cN0 only — no node-positive patients enrolled
what_we_know: Foundational NAC RCT in MIBC (MVAC → RC)
Established OS benefit for NAC in N0 MIBC
what_we_dont_know: Any direct evidence for cN1 M0 — not enrolled

trials.setting: Neoadjuvant CMV before definitive local treatment
trials.interpretation: Second foundational NAC trial; no cN+ patients.

what_we_know: Second foundational NAC trial (CMV); n=976 N0

subgroup_outcomes.headline: N0 only
what_we_know: Second foundational NAC trial (CMV); n=976 N0

trials.cn1_cn_plus_label: cN+ from retrospective series within reviews
trials.interpretation: Underpins standard NAC recommendation. cN+ data indirect.

what_we_know: OS HR ~0.82–0.87 for NAC in MIBC broadly

subgroup_outcomes.headline: Meta-analysis — cN+ not isolated
what_we_know: OS HR ~0.82–0.87 for NAC in MIBC broadly

trials.interpretation: Led to EV-302; activity/tolerability background only.

what_we_know: Led to EV-302; ORR 64.5% in la/mUC

subgroup_outcomes.headline: Metastatic cis-ineligible — not perioperative cN1
what_we_know: Led to EV-302; ORR 64.5% in la/mUC

trials.interpretation: Backup plan after surgery if pN+ persists. Not neoadjuvant argument.

what_we_know: Adjuvant nivolumab post-RC; DFS HR 0.70
143 patients with pN1 at surgery
what_we_dont_know: Neoadjuvant cN1 M0 pathway — different clinical question

subgroup_outcomes.headline: pN1 after surgery (adjuvant, not neoadjuvant)
what_we_know: Adjuvant nivolumab post-RC; DFS HR 0.70
143 patients with pN1 at surgery
what_we_dont_know: Neoadjuvant cN1 M0 pathway — different clinical question

trials.cn1_cn_plus_label: pN+ eligible — exact n not in primary
trials.interpretation: Adjuvant pembro option; DFS benefit, OS immature.

patient_match.n_label: pN+ eligible post-surgery; exact pN1 n not in primary
what_we_know: Adjuvant pembrolizumab; DFS benefit in high-risk MIBC
what_we_dont_know: Exact pN1 count; neoadjuvant cN1 data

subgroup_outcomes.headline: pN+ eligible post-surgery; exact pN1 n not in primary
what_we_know: Adjuvant pembrolizumab; DFS benefit in high-risk MIBC
what_we_dont_know: Exact pN1 count; neoadjuvant cN1 data

trials.setting: Perioperative EV+pembro vs neoadjuvant GC → RC (cisplatin-ELIGIBLE)
trials.cn1_cn_plus_label: cN1 included; subgroup n not yet reported (~5% of 808 estimated)

efficacy.subgroup_note: EFS, OS, pCR benefits consistent across pre-specified subgroups including clinical stage; granular cN1 data pending

trials.limitation_for_patient: cN1 subgroup size not yet published; FDA priority review pending (action date Aug 2026)
trials.interpretation: First phase 3 perioperative EV+pembro vs standard GC in CISPLATIN-ELIGIBLE MIBC including cN1 M0. Game-changer if she is cisplatin-fit — directly addresses prior evidence gap.

patient_match.n_label: cN1 M0 included (~5% estimated; not yet reported)
what_we_know: Phase 3 perioperative EV+pembro vs GC in cisplatin-ELIGIBLE MIBC
Eligibility: T2–T4aN0M0 or T1–T4aN1M0
Overall n=808: EFS HR 0.53, pCR 55.8% vs 32.5%
Benefits reported consistent across pre-specified subgroups including stage
what_we_dont_know: Exact cN1 patient count and dedicated cN1 EFS/OS/pCR

subgroup_outcomes.headline: cN1 M0 included (~5% estimated; not yet reported)
what_we_know: Phase 3 perioperative EV+pembro vs GC in cisplatin-ELIGIBLE MIBC
Eligibility: T2–T4aN0M0 or T1–T4aN1M0
Overall n=808: EFS HR 0.53, pCR 55.8% vs 32.5%
Benefits reported consistent across pre-specified subgroups including stage
what_we_dont_know: Exact cN1 patient count and dedicated cN1 EFS/OS/pCR

trials.setting: Induction MVAC or GC → RC in clinically node-positive MIBC
trials.limitation_for_patient: Retrospective; chemotherapy not EV+pembro; no randomization
trials.interpretation: Largest multi-institutional cN+ NAC series. 48% achieve pN0 after chemo; cN1 patients 56% pN0. Supports NAC before RC in node-positive disease.

what_we_know: 304 clinically node-positive patients; induction MVAC/GC → RC
48% overall pN0; 56% in cN1 subgroup
Median OS longer in downstaged patients

subgroup_outcomes.headline: All cN1–N3 M0; cN1-specific pN0 56%
what_we_know: 304 clinically node-positive patients; induction MVAC/GC → RC
48% overall pN0; 56% in cN1 subgroup
Median OS longer in downstaged patients

trials.setting: NAC vs upfront RC in cN+ MIBC (population-based)
trials.interpretation: Population data: NAC associated with 53% lower death risk in cN+ MIBC; cN1 3-y OS 66% vs 37% with upfront RC.

what_we_know: NAC associated with lower death risk (HR 0.47) in cN+ MIBC
cN1: 3-year OS 66% with NAC vs 37% upfront RC

subgroup_outcomes.headline: cN+ M0 population-based cohort
what_we_know: NAC associated with lower death risk (HR 0.47) in cN+ MIBC
cN1: 3-year OS 66% with NAC vs 37% upfront RC

trials.setting: Induction chemotherapy in clinically node-positive bladder cancer
trials.interpretation: 40.8% complete nodal response after induction chemo vs 11% surgery alone. Post-chemo node size on CT predicts residual nodal disease.

what_we_know: 40.8% pN0 after induction chemo vs 11.1% surgery alone
Median OS 12.8 y if pN0 vs 1.9 y if residual pN+

subgroup_outcomes.headline: Clinically node-positive MIBC
what_we_know: 40.8% pN0 after induction chemo vs 11.1% surgery alone
Median OS 12.8 y if pN0 vs 1.9 y if residual pN+

trials.setting: NAC + RC in cTanyN1-3M0 bladder cancer
trials.interpretation: 35% ypN0 at RC after NAC in cN+ patients; 5-y OS 34% overall.

trials.population: cT2-4a N0 M0 MIBC (cis-ineligible/refused); 2 patients cN1 in initial cohort

trials.interpretation: Pembro monotherapy neoadjuvant benchmark. ypN+ patients had only 41.9% 5-y OS — underscores nodal response importance.

trials.setting: 1L nivo + GC vs GC in metastatic UC

trials.interpretation: Chemo-IO also active in LN-only metastatic disease; superseded by EV-302 in 1L.
